meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no understanding of APIs (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve No API Knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to build seamless user onboarding without complex API knowledge. An expert-led guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You don't need deep API expertise to build high-converting onboarding flows. By leveraging Claude Code to generate modular logic and Antigravity to abstract state management, you can ship professional, data-driven onboarding experiences in hours. Focus on UI-first architecture to deliver client value while bypassing complex backend integration hurdles.
content.intro:
The client is waiting, the deadline is breathing down your neck, and the documentation for that third-party API looks like an ancient, undecipherable scroll. You’re a freelancer, not a backend architect, and you’re tired of losing billable hours fighting with REST endpoints just to get a simple user profile initialized.

But here is the secret the pros won't tell you: you don't need to master the guts of an API to build a world-class onboarding experience. By treating onboarding as a state-driven UI sequence rather than an API orchestration nightmare, you can ship faster, impress your clients, and secure your reputation as a high-velocity developer.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the user journey. We use Antigravity to generate a clean state machine that handles transitions without needing direct API calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean TypeScript state machine for a 3-step user onboarding flow (Welcome, Profile Setup, Preferences). Include logic for transition validation and state persistence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a state machine interface for onboarding in src/lib/onboarding.ts'

steps.title: Scaffold the UI Components
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build the view layer. By keeping the UI decoupled from the data layer, you can test the flow without needing a backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create reusable React components for an onboarding wizard. Use Tailwind CSS for styling and ensure each step is a modular component.

steps.title: Implement Mock Data Providers
steps.description: Avoid API fatigue by creating a local provider that simulates API responses. This lets you build the frontend perfectly before connecting to real endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock service layer that simulates API latency and success/error states for user profile data submission.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a mock data service to simulate onboarding API calls'

steps.title: Integrate Persistence Layer
steps.description: Use Claude Code to swap your mock service for a real API client once the UI is polished. This keeps your development velocity high.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the existing mock service to use Axios for POST requests, keeping the same method signatures to ensure zero breaking changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --target 'src/services/onboarding.ts' --action 'replace mock with real API'

steps.title: Error Handling and Edge Cases
steps.description: Finalize the build by adding robust error handling. Claude Code excels at identifying potential points of failure in your onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the onboarding flow for potential race conditions or state inconsistencies. Add graceful error messaging.

pro_tips: Always build the 'Happy Path' first; clients care more about the visual flow than the backend plumbing in the early stages.
Use local storage during initial development to persist state so you don't lose progress during browser refreshes.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your state machine; this prevents regressions when you finally connect to real APIs.
Keep your API calls in a dedicated 'Service' directory to make future maintenance or switching providers trivial.
If an API documentation is confusing, ask Claude to 'explain this endpoint like I am a frontend developer' to simplify the integration logic.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, focus on tracking user drop-off rates using a simple analytics hook to provide your clients with data-backed optimization insights.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting documentation and start shipping features that matter. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
